gaps.have: raw input/output/cache_creation/cache_read via Claude API usage + CostOps token_usage
gaps.need: tag tokens by APG origin: system_and_tools / inherited_context / apg_packet_fresh / on_demand_fresh / cache_creation / cache_read / output
gaps.risk_if_skipped: cannot measure the 3000/5000 fresh-input budget targets; APG context-budget policy is unenforceable

gaps.capability: commit -> build -> deploy -> runtime evidence chain
gaps.have: git commits, Render deploy-history API, /health runtime (as separate sources)
gaps.need: link them per change into one execution-receipt (tested_commit -> build_digest -> deployment -> runtime_smoke)
gaps.risk_if_skipped: release_ready gate 'tested_build_deploy_commit_chain_consistent' cannot be checked deterministically

gaps.have: origin_note on the bus (self-declared, unauthenticated, card 06f062e4)
gaps.need: structured actor_class/tenant_class/data_class/test_run_id/environment carried through the trace; KPI-exclusion + billing/comms suppress + cleanup for test identities
gaps.risk_if_skipped: P0.A test-identity isolation cannot be asserted; test runs can pollute KPIs / trigger real comms
gaps.related: authenticated-identity is a separate UPSTREAM hardening (GAP-APG-06)

gaps.need: expose CostOps as the provider/capacity resolver the methodology delegates to (agent-routing.yaml: 'provider_resolution delegated to CostOps/capacity router'); methodology stores capability class only
gaps.risk_if_skipped: capability-class -> concrete provider resolution stays ad-hoc (tonight's DeepSeek/Max/gpt-5.6-sol routing was manual)

gaps.have: ad-hoc research/market deliverable files under agents/*/deliverables
gaps.need: a queryable intel registry (market/competitor/VoC) the methodology can reference by id instead of loading full documents (context-budget rule: 'use references first')
gaps.risk_if_skipped: research context cannot be reference-pulled within the token budget; S3 research packets bloat

gaps.have: Render production + preview envs (preview cost-excluded) + manual reversible rollback
gaps.need: a risk-based staging/canary stage for safe-release profile (production synthetic canary for high-risk); low-risk stays smoke+rollback
gaps.risk_if_skipped: safe-release profile high-risk path (synthetic canary) has no substrate; only smoke+rollback available

gaps.have: each product has landing/demo/(pre)registration/login/app but in different launch states
gaps.need: a per-product surface descriptor APG reads (not one assumed shape); already partially implied by product configs
gaps.risk_if_skipped: product-surface P0.B checks assume a uniform shape that does not exist

note: Zero BLOCKED. All gaps are ADAPTER (thin mapping, no new infra) or UPSTREAM_GAP (shared-layer build) or PRODUCT_LOCAL (per-product read). The shadow replay can run today without closing any gap; the gaps determine what a REAL (non-shadow) APG run would need.
